title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 5, 2026
headline: Bolivia's sixth consecutive week of lethal protest repression produces a −5.6 downgrade and a new STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX methodology ruling, while the United States' open 06-04 Developing-to-Critical proposal deepens as ICE rescinds its post-release death-reporting requirement and Yemen records a WFP staffer death in Houthi detention.
summary: One change proposal this cycle. Bolivia (28.4→22.8, −5.6, remains Developing) is the only scored movement: six weeks of nationwide protests against the worst economic crisis in four decades met with lethal state force — 4 protesters killed, 127+ detained, and terrorism plus narco-funding charges filed against COB labor-federation chief Mario Argullo and 24 union leaders. The directly-attested deaths and detentions reveal un-priced ACC, BND, EQU, EMP, and INT harm on a non-floor Developing entity, producing a defensible multi-dimension downgrade that clears the five-point threshold while remaining within the Developing band. A new methodology ruling — STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX (SCORABLE) — formalizes that new state-attributable lethal repression of dissent is scorable on a Developing entity even absent apparatus collapse; the ruling is distinguished from the conflict-driven 0.0 floors, from filed-but-unadjudicated corporate matters, and from the same-cycle duplicate-suppression applied to the United States. The United States' 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical) is reinforced but not duplicated: ICE's rescission of the 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement is same-regime continuation that deepens the proposed ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor. Yemen records a new gravity marker — a WFP staffer died in Houthi detention, amid the UNSC's June 6 unanimous demand for release of 73 UN and civil-society staff; WFP has terminated 365 staff contracts; the ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR designation holds. The Democratic Republic of Congo's Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak has accelerated to 515 cases and 91 deaths (from 381/64 on June 4) with Uganda spread continuing and CDC's worst-case scenario reaching 20,000; contact tracing has fallen to 20% coverage. Seven absolute-floor confirmations this cycle — Russia, Myanmar, Sudan, Yemen, Afghanistan, Syria, Israel — with two sub-threshold corporate boundary cases carrying forward: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical ten days from the WARN Act June 15 deadline, and 3M at 0.3 points above Critical on two concurrent sovereign PFAS suits.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 28.4 → 22.8 (−5.6) — STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX: Lethal Force and Criminalization of Labor Leadership in Sixth Week of Protests
topSignals.description: Bolivia's nationwide protests against an acute economic crisis — triggered by a land-mortgage law affecting indigenous and worker communities — entered their sixth consecutive week with state-attributable deaths and mass detention. Four protesters were killed and more than 127 detained in the most recent documented period; subsequent reporting through June 9 documents 10 killed and 365 arrested, confirming the proposal magnitude is conservative. Terrorism and narco-funding charges were filed against COB national labor-federation executive secretary Mario Argullo and 24 union leaders — an AB1 deflection and AB4 weaponization of the justice apparatus against organized civil society. The Defense and Education Ministers resigned June 2, indicating a functioning-but-stressed state apparatus rather than collapse. Bolivia is a non-floor Developing entity; the attested deaths and detentions reveal un-priced harm across five dimensions: ACC (criminalizing dissent, 1.8→1.4), BND (lethal force against civilians, 2.3→1.9), EQU (repression of indigenous and worker claimants, 1.9→1.6), EMP (2.4→2.0), and INT (justice-apparatus weaponization, 2.0→1.7). AWR, ACT, and SYS are held — the apparatus is stressed, not collapsed. The multi-dimension downgrade of −5.6 (28.4→22.8) clears the five-point change-proposal threshold. No band crossing: Bolivia remains in the Developing band, well above the Critical boundary at 20/21. A new methodology ruling — STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX (SCORABLE) — establishes that new state-attributable lethal repression of dissent is scorable on a non-floor Developing entity even when the apparatus has not collapsed, distinguishing this from the conflict-driven floor entities and the filed-but-unadjudicated corporate cluster.

topSignals.title: United States 23.4 — ICE Death-Reporting Rescission Reinforces Open 06-04 Proposal; Duplicate-Suppression Discipline Applied
topSignals.description: The June 5 signal — ICE's rescission of the 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement, the 17th detention death of 2026 (approximately one every six days), and a 65% collapse in Fortune 500 DEI disclosures — is same-regime continuation that reinforces the pending 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical) without materially changing the −5.9 magnitude. The 06-04 proposal is grounded in Tier-5 adjudicated judicial findings by Judge Murphy (D. Mass.) and Judge Cobb (D.D.C.) ruling core deportation architecture unlawful — a conduct layer the June 5 evidence deepens but does not alter. No duplicate proposal was created; the June 5 ICE rescission is logged as corroborating evidence for the open proposal. The 06-04 proposal remains the authoritative open watch for the United States: the proposed 17.5 Critical composite is the lower-confidence reading, and the documented evidence pattern — including the June 5 transparency rollback — is the higher-confidence record.

topSignals.title: Yemen 0.0 — WFP Staffer Dies in Houthi Detention; 73 UN Staff Held; WFP Terminates 365 Contracts
topSignals.description: A World Food Programme staff member died in Houthi detention, adding a new gravity marker to the ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR designation confirmed on June 4. The UNSC unanimously condemned the detentions on June 6 and demanded immediate release of 73 UN and civil-society staff held since 2024. HRW and Amnesty International issued a joint demand for release on June 7. WFP has terminated 365 staff contracts in response to the detention pattern. The in-custody death deepens the Houthi-perpetrated in-territory harm the 0.0 floor already encodes; the ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR ruling holds and cannot move lower. More than 18.3 million people in Yemen are food-insecure. First improvement signals would be verified release of all detained staff and confirmed humanitarian-corridor opening.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 — Ebola Bundibugyo Accelerates to 515/91; Uganda Spread; CDC Worst-Case 20,000; Contact Tracing at 20%
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak has reached 515 cases and 91 deaths — up from 381 cases and 64 deaths on June 4. Uganda spread continues with 19 cases and 2 deaths. The CDC has modeled a worst-case scenario of 20,000 cases. Contact tracing coverage has declined to approximately 20% of known transmission chains. The PHEIC declared by WHO remains active; no approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. DRC carries a concurrent 26.5 million food-insecure population. The NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT ruling sustains the near-floor 2.3 composite: the outbreak reveals already-priced state-response incapacity, absent documented deliberate state obstruction. The conversion trigger is documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, or case-data suppression.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Act Deadline Now 10 Days Out; Boundary at 0.6 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window for approximately 30,000 affected workers closes June 15 — ten days from tonight's cycle. The June 15 date materializes an already-priced layoff; the scored adjustment, if warranted, would arise from adjudicated findings on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory. The Washington state class action and New Jersey state WARN 90-day proceedings are the two active adjudication channels. Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical — the second-narrowest active corporate margin after 3M's 0.3-point boundary. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — 612 Executions YTD; June 1 Secret Executions of Kurdish Prisoners; June 3 Execution of January Protester
topSignals.description: Iran executed Kurdish prisoners Maleki and Mohammadinia in secret on June 1 and Mehdi Avari — convicted in connection with the January protest cycle — on June 3. The 2026 YTD execution count has reached 612, with 123-week hunger strikes documented at 56 detention facilities and 41 or more protesters at identified imminent execution risk. The SCALE-FLOOR ruling holds at 2.5: the June execution surge deepens a single dominant extremity already priced in the composite and does not satisfy the multi-category threshold required for movement to the absolute 0.0 floor. The scale-floor anchor is reinforced.

topSignals.title: 3M Company 20.3 — Boundary at 0.3 Points Above Critical; Australia $1.43B and Minnesota MPCA Suits Carry Forward
topSignals.description: 3M's published composite of 20.3 sits 0.3 points above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. The Australia $1.43B sovereign PFAS claim and Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit (filed within one month of each other; carry-forward from June 4) remain the active adjudication channels. A modeled downgrade of −2.3 would cross into Critical but falls below the five-point change-proposal threshold. Prior settled reparation ($850M Minnesota 2018; A$408M Australian community settlements) holds ACC above the absolute floor under the FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED plus ADJUDICATED-PRIOR-REPARATION framing. The decisive conversion triggers are adverse adjudication or settlement of either suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense in the Minnesota proceeding.

topSignals.title: India 15.6 — Deportee Count Reaches ~5,000; 145 Reported Disappearances Logged as Watch Item
topSignals.description: The expulsion of Bengali Muslims to Bangladesh has escalated from 1,500 to approximately 5,000 deportees, with HRW documenting citizens expelled without verification, abuse at gunpoint, and 145 reportedly disappeared. The June 5 escalation deepens an EQU 1.3, EMP 1.4, ACC 1.4 profile established by the applied June 1 downgrade (22.7→15.6); it reinforces rather than reveals new un-priced harm and does not generate a second proposal. The 145 disappearance reports are a forward watch item: if confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale, a further scored movement beyond the 15.6 baseline becomes warranted.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions is the primary reassessment signal. ICE in-custody death trajectory and reinstatement of the rescinded 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement are the leading continuity indicators.
boundaryWatch.note: United States sits 3.4 points below the Developing/Critical boundary at the proposed 17.5 composite. The band crossing rests on Tier-5 adjudicated judicial findings (Murphy Feb 25; Cobb D.D.C.) that the core deportation architecture is unlawful. The June 5 ICE death-reporting rescission deepens the documented conduct pattern without altering the proposed magnitude.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense in the Minnesota proceeding, converts the sub-threshold −2.3 modeled downgrade to a scored change proposal into Critical.
boundaryWatch.note: 3M sits 0.3 points above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. Two concurrent sovereign PFAS suits (Australia $1.43B; Minnesota MPCA PFOS at 310,000 ppt vs 0.05 ppt standard) raise enforcement density. Prior settled reparation ($850M Minnesota; A$408M Australian community) holds ACC above the floor.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15 — 10 days from this cycle. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and Washington class action are the active adjudication channels on the remote-reclassification avoidance theory.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6 points above Critical. The June 15 WARN deadline is the most proximate known hard trigger date across all assessed entities this cycle. Adverse WARN-avoidance adjudication would likely cross into Critical from the current baseline.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of humanitarian access, a state-attributable mass-casualty event, or confirmation of famine areas in Nigeria convert the PRICED-CONTINUATION carry-forward to a scorable downgrade.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 21.9 is 1.9 points above Critical. Approximately 34.7 million lean-season food-insecure people and 3.5 million IDPs reflect non-state armed group and structural-fragility drivers already priced at the 21.9 baseline. The PRICED-CONTINUATION/NON-STATE-DRIVEN ruling holds absent documented deliberate state obstruction of aid.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Verified delivery of the UK CMA publisher opt-out and attribution remedy (approximately nine months) plus replication in EU or another major jurisdiction constitutes the upgrade trigger. EU AI Act GPAI enforcement takes full effect August 2.
boundaryWatch.note: Alphabet/Google remains at the exact 40.0/41 Developing-Functional boundary. The COMPELLED-REMEDY-NOT-SELF-CORRECTION ruling established June 3 holds. Upgrade condition is operationalized delivery, not announced intention.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth — a favorable ruling credits the DoD-terms refusal as an I1 governance signal and triggers the Functional/Established crossing from 59.1.
boundaryWatch.note: Cycle 17. DC Circuit oral arguments remain divided with no ruling issued. Anthropic's holdout from the DoW classified-network access framework continues as a standing governance distinction from peer AI labs.

emergingRisks.risk: Bolivia's protest repression has already exceeded the June 5 modeled magnitude — subsequent reporting through June 9 documents 10 killed and 365 arrested — deepening the STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX pattern within the Developing band.
emergingRisks.detail: The June 5 proposal is conservative at −5.6 (28.4→22.8). The June 9 count of 10 killed and 365 arrested represents a 2.5x increase in both fatalities and detentions from the modeled baseline. Confirmed extrajudicial-killing findings, adjudication of terrorism charges against labor leaders, or cessation of negotiations will each constitute forward reassessment triggers. Bolivia remains in Developing; further documented lethal force could widen the gap from Critical.

emergingRisks.risk: The United States' open 06-04 Developing-to-Critical proposal accumulates corroborating evidence each cycle; the ICE death-reporting rescission documents a transparency-degradation pattern that compounds the already-proposed ACC 1.4 floor.
emergingRisks.detail: The pending 06-04 proposal (23.4→17.5, Developing→Critical) is grounded in Tier-5 judicial findings by Murphy and Cobb. The June 5 ICE rescission of the 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement is a documented transparency rollback within the same conduct pattern. The 17th detention death reinforces the approximately one-death-every-six-days trajectory. The proposed 17.5 composite is the lower-confidence reading; the accumulating evidence record is the higher-confidence signal.

emergingRisks.risk: The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak is accelerating faster than the 06-04 trajectory: 515 cases / 91 deaths (up from 381/64 in 24 hours); Uganda urban spread; 20% contact tracing; CDC worst-case 20,000.
emergingRisks.detail: The outbreak trajectory has worsened materially between the June 4 and June 5 cycles. Contact-tracing coverage has fallen from 45% to 20%. The CDC's modeled worst-case of 20,000 cases is now the operative planning scenario. The NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold rests on the absence of documented deliberate state obstruction; any confirmed access denial, fund diversion, or data suppression would convert the near-floor 2.3 composite to an active-harm scoring case.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle sits 0.6 points above Critical with the WARN Act sign-or-forfeit window closing June 15 — the most proximate hard trigger date across all assessed entities.
emergingRisks.detail: Approximately 30,000 workers are in the WARN window. The remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is the conversion mechanism from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline. NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are both active. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding on the avoidance theory before or after June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: India's 145 reported disappearances among ~5,000 deportees document a potential new category of state-attributable harm beyond the priced refoulement and expulsion pattern.
emergingRisks.detail: The applied June 1 downgrade (22.7→15.6) prices the discriminatory mass-expulsion pattern. The 145 reportedly disappeared are a watch item not yet scored: if confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale, this constitutes a new harm category beyond the priced EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile and would be independently scorable. The EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12, adding a structural enforcement layer relevant to India's non-refoulement posture.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU Returns Regulation enters force June 12 — seven days from this cycle — creating structured enforcement mechanisms for third-country return procedures consequential for entities with standing non-refoulement profiles.
emergingRisks.detail: India (15.6 Critical, applied June 1), Jordan (35.9 rotation confirm, refugee-hosting strain), and Romania (Developing, EU-member return hub) each carry standing non-refoulement or minority-return profiles. Any documented EU-facilitated return to a country where a third-country national faces persecution without individual assessment converts from watch to a scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect August 2 — 58 days from this cycle — with no assessed AI lab having published compliance documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines reach €35M or 7% of global annual turnover. The compliance gap is structural across the full assessed AI labs cluster. OpenAI's comprehensive full assessment remains overdue. The EU AI Act August 2 date is accumulating as each cycle passes without disclosed compliance documentation.

emergingRisks.risk: The West Africa and Sahel lean season is tracking 52.8 million food-insecure — the regional humanitarian load continues building toward the August peak with funding gaps across the full cluster.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan and South Sudan sustain dual-country CONFLICT-DRIVEN-FAMINE-AT-FLOOR designation. Nigeria at 21.9 (1.9pt above Critical) carries approximately 34.7 million lean-season food-insecure under the PRICED-CONTINUATION/NON-STATE-DRIVEN ruling. Further IPC Phase 5 area expansion is probable through the August lean-season peak at current funding levels.

STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX (SCORABLE) ruling formalizes that attested state lethal force against protesters is scorable on a non-floor Developing entity absent apparatus collapse — distinguished from conflict-driven 0.0 floors, filed-but-unadjudicated corporate matters, and same-cycle duplicate-suppression

methodologyNotes.name: STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX (SCORABLE)
methodologyNotes.description: New ruling established this cycle for Bolivia. Where a non-floor Developing entity supplies directly-attested new state-attributable lethal force against protesters, mass detention, and criminalization of organized civil society leadership — and the state apparatus is demonstrated to be functioning-but-stressed rather than collapsed — the evidence is scorable as a multi-dimension downgrade. Bolivia (4 killed, 127+ detained, terrorism charges against COB labor chief Argullo and 24 union leaders) is the anchor case. Distinguishing features: (1) the entity is not at the 0.0 floor — harm is new and un-priced rather than reinforcing an existing absolute floor; (2) the harm is directly-attested via Tier 2-3 reporting of fatalities and detentions, not filed-but-unadjudicated; (3) the state apparatus retains functioning institutions (ministerial resignations are stress signals, not collapse indicators). Dimension moves: ACC (criminalizing-dissent), BND (lethal-force boundary violation), EQU (repression of indigenous/worker claimants), EMP, INT (justice-apparatus weaponization); AWR/ACT/SYS held. First application at the scored-proposal level; previously introduced at sub-threshold on 06-03.

methodologyNotes.name: SAME-REGIME-CONTINUATION / DUPLICATE-SUPPRESSED
methodologyNotes.description: Extended ruling applied this cycle for the United States. Where a pending open proposal already prices a conduct pattern and new same-cycle evidence is assessed as same-regime continuation rather than a materially new category of harm, no duplicate proposal is created. The new evidence is logged as corroborating evidence for the open proposal. United States (ICE death-reporting rescission reinforcing the pending 06-04 proposal's ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor) is the cycle's application. Distinguished from Bolivia: the US evidence is same-regime continuation of a pattern already priced in an open proposal; Bolivia's June 5 evidence supplies a NEW scorable harm category on a baseline that had no pending proposal. First applied for the United States on 06-05; the principle was established 06-04.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED-IN-RECENT-ADJUSTMENT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for India. Where an applied recent downgrade has set a new baseline and subsequent evidence escalates within the same pattern (rather than revealing a new harm category), the evidence reinforces rather than reveals un-priced harm. India (22.7→15.6 applied June 1; June 5 escalation to ~5,000 deportees within the same mass-expulsion pattern) is the cycle's application. The 145 reportedly disappeared are categorically distinct from the priced refoulement/expulsion pattern and are logged as a watch item rather than a priced continuation. Converts to a new proposal if confirmed as state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for DRC. Ebola Bundibugyo (515/91; PHEIC; Uganda spread; CDC worst-case 20,000; 20% contact tracing) is an exogenous shock revealing priced state-response incapacity, absent documented deliberate state obstruction. DRC confirms at 2.3. The overlay holds unless documented deliberate state interference with the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — is confirmed. The acceleration of the outbreak (from 381/64 on 06-04 to 515/91 on 06-05) does not independently break the overlay; the conversion trigger is deliberate state conduct, not outbreak trajectory.

methodologyNotes.name: EXOGENOUS-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT (Strait of Hormuz)
methodologyNotes.description: New disambiguation established this cycle. The Strait of Hormuz closure (Brent peaked $126/bbl; 45M additional people at food-security risk) is an exogenous shock affecting multiple entities simultaneously — Afghanistan, Syria, Sudan, Jordan. The Hormuz shock is explicitly non-double-counted: it is treated as an exogenous overlay revealing priced incapacity or fragility, not as independent state-attributable harm for each affected entity. All four entities assessed with this disambiguation sustain their published baselines.

methodologyNotes.name: ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for Yemen. A WFP staffer died in Houthi detention — a new gravity marker within the in-territory Houthi-perpetrated harm already encoded by the absolute 0.0 floor. The ATTRIBUTION-NON-STATE-ACTOR-AT-FLOOR ruling holds: Houthi in-territory conduct is encoded by the active-perpetration and conflict-driven floor; the country entity aggregates all in-territory actors at the floor. Yemen confirmed at 0.0; the detainee death reinforces but does not require a new proposal.

methodologyNotes.name: FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED plus ADJUDICATED-PRIOR-REPARATION
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed this cycle for 3M Company and Oracle Corporation. Neither the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim nor the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit has reached merits adjudication; Oracle's WARN-avoidance theory is pre-adjudication. Prior settled reparation for 3M ($850M Minnesota 2018; A$408M Australian community) holds ACC above the floor. Both entities carry-forward at published baselines. Conversion to scored proposals requires adverse adjudication or settlement (3M) or adjudicated WARN-avoidance finding (Oracle).

forwardTriggers.trigger: EU Returns Regulation enters force; documented EU-facilitated return to a country where a third-country national faces persecution without individual assessment constitutes a scored signal under the RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT anchor. India at 15.6 and Jordan at 35.9 are the primary non-refoulement watch entities.

WARN Act last-working-day window closes for approximately 30,000 affected workers. Adjudicated finding on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in NJ state proceedings or the Washington class action constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from the 0.6-point-above-Critical baseline.

UNSC UNAMA mandate renewal vote. Non-renewal reduces accountability architecture without altering the entity's own conduct trajectory. Reversal of Decree No.12 or Decree No.18 constitutes the first scored improvement signal from the absolute floor.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July with confirmed Kampala transmission and accelerating trajectory (515/91 from 381/64 in 24 hours). Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite. Phase-2 MDL deadline also falls July 31 for 3M.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to €35M or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation. OpenAI comprehensive full assessment overdue at 52+ days.

West and Central Africa lean season peak. Sudan and South Sudan sustain floor designation. Nigeria (21.9) at 1.9pt above Critical; further IPC Phase 5 area expansion or documented state obstruction of aid converts to a scorable downgrade.

Confirmed extrajudicial-killing findings, mass-casualty escalation, or charge adjudication may warrant further movement within Developing. Negotiated settlement, charge withdrawal, or casualty cessation constitutes a reversal signal toward the 28.4 baseline. The June 9 report of 10 killed and 365 arrested already exceeds the modeled magnitude.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Further ICE in-custody deaths reinforce the proposed 17.5 Critical baseline. Reinstatement of the rescinded 30-day post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction signal within the open proposal.

Confirmation of 145+ state-attributable enforced disappearances at scale beyond the priced refoulement pattern. Judicial findings on wrongful citizen expulsion also constitute a potential trigger for further scored movement from the applied 15.6 baseline.

Adverse adjudication or settlement of the Australia $1.43B PFAS claim or the Minnesota MPCA PFOS suit, or rejection of 3M's military-contractor federal immunity defense. A disciplined modeled downgrade of −2.3 would cross from the 0.3-point-above-Critical baseline into Critical.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the three converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA-billing criminal probe, MA AG antitrust suit, AZ AG eight-insurer price-fixing cartel suit — is the leading corporate scoring trigger of the current cycle.
DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth (Pentagon supply-chain-risk designation). Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If Bolivia's ACC and BND dimensional scores decline on attested lethal force (4 killed, 127+ detained) under the STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX ruling, does a subsequent negotiated resolution between the government and COB leadership — without any adjudication of the terrorism charges — constitute sufficient evidence to reverse those dimensional scores toward the 28.4 baseline, or does the attested lethal conduct hold independently of charge outcomes?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: The STATE-REPRESSION-IN-FLUX ruling scores on attested deaths and detentions, not charge outcomes — but the methodology has not yet specified whether a political settlement extinguishing the charges also extinguishes the scored dimensional harm.
